title: asphodelus bento-rainhae subsp. salmanticus
intro: Asphodelus bento-rainhae subsp. salmanticus, also known as a subspecies of the asphodel, is a type of plant in the Asphodelaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Asphodelus bento-rainhae subsp. salmanticus include reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and soothing sore throats. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
